rulings.Case Title: EDDIE SUMMERFIELD VS. GUIZHEN LUO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Leave / Motion To Take Notice Of, And Enter [Proposed/Attached] Notice Of Lien On Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 6, 2018, Line 3, CLAIMANT ADIL HIRAMANEK's Leave / Motion To Take Notice Of, And Enter [Proposed/Attached] Notice Of Lien On Judgment Purported lien claimant Adil Hiramanek's request for a court order for a "right to attach" lien to the judgment is denied. The sole authority cited for Mr. Hiramenek's request is CCP 491.410, but that code section only applies if the purported lien claimant (here Mr. Hiramenek) has already obtained an attachment, which Mr. Hiramenek has not done. To the extent that Hiramenek is seeking an attachment, he has failed to provide admissible evidence establishing the probable validity of his claim that plaintiff Eddie Summerfield owes him 50% of the judgment entered in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. WILLIAM T TAYLOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Defualt Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 6, 2018, Line 18, DEFENDANT WILLIAM TAYLOR's Motion To Set Aside Default And Defualt Judgment Defendant William Taylor's motion to set aside the default entered against him on April 17, 2017 and the default judgment entered against him on April 22, 2018 is granted. The default and default judgment are set aside. Mr. Taylor's additional motion to quash service of summons is denied. CACH has not submitted sufficient evidence to refute Mr. Taylor's statement under penalty of perjury that he first learned of this lawsuit on or around July 16, 2018 and that he was not evading service. Those statements are sufficient to warrant setting aside the default and default judgment. Mr. Taylor must file his answer no later than September 14, 2018. Mr. Taylor has not shown that the service of summons should be quashed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY A BONNICI VS. CHARLES MCMACKIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Judgment [Ccp Sec 473(B)]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 6, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY BONNICI Notice Of Motion For Relief From Judgment [Ccp Sec 473(B)] Plaintiff Timothy Bonnici's motion for relief from judgment is granted. The January 24, 2018 judgment is set aside and the case is restored to the civil active list. Mr. Bonnici is entitled to have the judgment set aside based on the mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Mr. Molinari's second declaration of fault adequately establishes that the January 24 judgment was entered against Mr. Bonnici due to the mistake and neglect of Mr. Molinari. In keeping with the remedial purposes of CCP 473(b), the court determines that, while this motion surely could have been filed earlier, relief should not be denied to Mr. Bonnici on the grounds that it was not filed within a reasonable time since the timing of the filing of the motion appears to have been determined by Mr. Molinari, not Mr. Bonnici. Although there is sometimes a fine line between "deliberate tactical choice" and attorney mistake and neglect, Mr. Molinari's errors are more accurately characterized as mistake and neglect than "deliberate tactical choice" and that characterization comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits especially where, as here, there is no assertion of legal prejudice by the non-moving party, nor does it appear that such an assertion could be credibly made. Defendants Carroll Henry and Charles McMackin are entitled to an award of fees and costs per CCP 473(b). There will be a further hearing on October 3, 2018 to determine the amount of that award. Defendants are required to submit a short memorandum and evidence stating the amount they are seeking no later than September 14, 2018. Mr. Bonnici must file any opposition papers no later than September 21, 2018. Defendants must file any reply papers by September 28, 2018. Defendants' demurrer to the complaint will also be re-calendared for October 3, 2018. Mr. Bonnici must file their opposition papers to the demurrer by September 21, 2018 and defendants must file any reply papers by September 28, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Bonnici is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: TERRY SCHENK VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Deposition Testimony And To Produce Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 6, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF TERRY SCHENK's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Deposition Testimony And To Produce Documents And Request For Sanctions Plaintiff Terry Schenk's motion to compel further responses to deposition testimony and to produce documents and for monetary sanctions is granted in small part. Danilo Celeste is required to submit to a further session of his deposition to provide further response to the question whether "After the subject incident, did you ever receive any disciplinary comments from any supervisor?" and reasonable follow-up questions. That question seeks directly relevant information and outweighs Mr. Celeste's legitimate privacy interests. None of the other questions which Mr. Celeste was instructed not to answer and none of the document requests seek directly relevant information (and, in many instances, not even information that is likely to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence) and thus the motion is denied as to all those questions and requests. Because defendant City and County of San Francisco's positions were substantially justified, Mr. Schenk's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CRAIG POWERS ET AL VS. DECLAN ROGERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Soren Lassen And Sharmila Lassen'S Motion To Compel Plainitff'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Fees, Costs And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney Of Record.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 6, 2018, Line 2, Defendants Soren Lassen And Sharmila Lassen's Motion To Compel Plainitff'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Fees, Costs And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney Of Record. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED: Plaintiff Craig Powers must provide a further, verified, written response to Interrogatories Nos. 59-62 (incorrectly enumerated as Nos. 58-61) hat specifically states the amounts Plaintiff actually paid for HOA fees of any kind during the specified years. Plaintiff has an obligation to diligently review any and all records in their possession or control to ascertain a fully responsive answer to the interrogatory, under penalty of perjury. The Court finds that Plaintiff's responses as well as his supplemental responses to date are evasive, despite the moving parties' efforts at meet and confer and accommodation of a lengthy period of time before bringing their motion. The Court awards sanctions against both the responding party Craig Powers and his attorneys of record herein in the total amount of $1,660.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK FLOYD VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 6, 2018, Line 12, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT Defendant Ford Motor Company's demurrer to the sixth cause of action for fraud by omission in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Patrick Floyd is sustained without leave to amend. Mr. Floyd was previously given leave to amend to plead specific facts showing his delayed discovery of Ford's alleged fraud. "In order to rely on the discovery rule for delayed accrual of a cause of action, [a] plaintiff whose complaint shows on its face that his claim would be barred without the benefit of the discovery rule must specifically plead facts to show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. In assessing the sufficiency of the allegations of delayed discovery, the court places the burden on the plaintiff to show diligence; conclusory allegations will not withstand demurrer." (Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808 (citation and internal quotation marks omitted)). As explained in Cansino v. Bank of America (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1472: "Because the discovery rule operates as an exception to the statute of limitations, if an action is brought more than three years after commission of the fraud, plaintiff has the burden of pleading and proving that he did not make the discovery until within three years prior to the filing of his complaint. To excuse failure to discover the fraud within three years after its commission, a plaintiff also must plead facts showing that he was not negligent in failing to make the discovery sooner and that he had no actual or presumptive knowledge of facts sufficient to put him on inquiry. To that end, a plaintiff must allege facts showing the time and surrounding circumstances of the discovery and what the discovery was. Conclusory allegations will not withstand a demurrer. The discovery related facts should be pleaded in detail to allow the court to determine whether the fraud should have been discovered sooner. (Citations and internal quotation marks omitted). The statute of limitations for a fraud claim is 3 years per CCP 338. Mr. Floyd's allegations show that he was on inquiry notice by early 2014 and he filed his complaint on September 6, 2017. The defects in the car existed at the time of the sale and began manifesting themselves in 2013. (Second Amended Complaint pars. 9 and 48.) Mr. Floyd's allegation at paragraph 50(c) where he states that he discovered the fraud in April of 2017 at the time of the buyback is conclusory. Mr. Floyd does not allege what specifically gave him reason to suspect fraud at that time rather than in 2014. Mr. Floyd's new allegations at paragraph 9 merely recount the car's repair history and, if anything, those allegations show that he had inquiry notice by January of 2014 as a matter of law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ford is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: OOLONG, LLC. VS. PETER ZUCCARELLI AND VACUUM COATING SUPPLIES, LLC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 6, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANT PETER ZUCCARELLI, VACUUM COATING SUPPLIES LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint Defendants' Peter Zuccarelli and Vacuum Coating Supplies, LLC's ("VCS") motion to quash is granted. Plaintiff Oolong, LLC has the burden of demonstrating sufficient minimum contacts to warrant the exercise of general or specific jurisdiction. In this case, plaintiff's evidence (the declarations of McElhenney and Castaldo) fail to show jurisdiction over VCS or Mr. Zuccarelli, who are both located in Texas. The evidence establishes that VCS passively advertised the Leybold Syrus 1105 machine for sale on e-Bay, plaintiff reached out to VCS to purchase the machine, plaintiff prepared its own purchase invoice and sent it to VCS in Texas, and the parties subsequently had communications regarding modifying the machine. (Zuccarelli Dec.) The court finds that it must grant the motion to quash because there is no salient difference between this action and Shisler v. Sanfer Sports Cars, Inc. (2006) 146 Cal.App.4th 1254. VCS initially posted the advertisement on e-Bay and it was not directly targeted towards California. Plaintiff thereafter reached out to VCS to purchase the machine. This case, like Shisler, involved a one-time transaction. The fact that the parties subsequently had numerous communications regarding the modifications to the machine does not change the character of the transaction from a one-off deal. Because plaintiff has no evidence to establish general jurisdiction and fails to establish the "purposeful availment" prong of the test for specific jurisdiction, the court grants the motion to quash. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JERILYNNE AKUTAGAWA VS. MARKEL AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 6, 2018, Line 15, DEFENDANT ROBERT PENA's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendant Robert Pena's demurrer to the second, third and fifth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jerilynne Akutagawa is sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and overruled as to the third and fifth causes of action for fraud. Ms. Akutagawa alleges that defendants negligently misrepresented that they would pay the $125,000. (FAC par. 36 (emphasis added)). A negligent misrepresentation cause of action cannot be based on a promise regarding future conduct. It must be made with respect to "a past or existing material fact." (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 152, 158.) Ms. Akutagawa does not meaningfully distinguish Tarmann. Ms. Akutagawa pleads her two fraud claims with the requisite specificity. Harlow v. American Equitable Ins. Co. (1927) 87 Cal.App. 28 does not mandate dismissal of the fraud claims. In Harlow it was clear that the parties had an insurance contract and the insured plaintiff failed to perform by giving timely notice and proof of loss. The court dismissed the fraud claim on demurrer because even if the insurer had a fraudulent intent to not perform the insurance policy, it did not matter because the insured was not put into a worse position. In this case, the alleged fraud put Ms. Akutagawa in a worse position because she did not receive the settlement funds and was induced into waiving the statute of limitations with respect to her wrongful death claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Pena is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JERILYNNE AKUTAGAWA VS. MARKEL AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 6, 2018, Line 15, DEFENDANTS MARKEL AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY and MARKEL INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendants Markel American Insurance Company and Markel Insurance Company's demurrer to the second, third and fifth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jerilynne Akutagawa is sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and overruled as to the third and fifth causes of action for fraud. Ms. Akutagawa alleges that defendants negligently misrepresented that they would pay the $125,000. (FAC par. 36 (emphasis added)). A negligent misrepresentation cause of action cannot be based on a promise regarding future conduct. It must be made with respect to "a past or existing material fact." (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 152, 158.) Ms. Akutagawa does not meaningfully distinguish Tarmann. Ms. Akutagawa pleads her two fraud claims with the requisite specificity. Harlow v. American Equitable Ins. Co. (1927) 87 Cal.App. 28 does not mandate dismissal of the fraud claims. In Harlow it was clear that the parties had an insurance contract and the insured plaintiff failed to perform by giving timely notice and proof of loss. The court dismissed the fraud claim on demurrer because even if the insurer had a fraudulent intent to not perform the insurance policy, it did not matter because the insured was not put into a worse position. In this case, the alleged fraud put Ms. Akutagawa in a worse position because she did not receive the settlement funds and was induced into waiving the statute of limitations with respect to her wrongful death claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Markel defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: 942 MARKET STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. 942 HCT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 6, 2018, Line 17, DEFENDANTS RCDC 942 MARKET STREET, LLC and RCDC 942 L.L.C's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Defendants RCDC 942 Market Street, LLC and RCDC 942 L.L.C.'s demurrer to the first through fourth and sixth through eighth causes of action in plaintiff 942 Market Street Owners' Association's complaint is: a) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to first through third, sixth and seventh causes of action for violation of residential building standards, negligence, strict liability, breach of fiduciary duty and violation of governing documents and b) sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth and eighth causes of action for breach of implied warranty of habitability and unlawful LLC distributions. Because there are five related builder defendants, each with what appears to be a different role regarding the real property that is the subject of this case, as to each cause of action 942 Market Street Owners' Association should allege why it contends each of these demurring defendants is liable instead of lumping the builder defendants all together in a collectively defined term. The Association should add facts explaining how the demurring defendants are liable since they were not in existence when the project was constructed and are not parties to the CC&Rs. Defendants' remaining arguments on the first cause of action lack merit. Civil Code 930(b), which discusses staying an action, indicates that the failure to comply with the notice/repair requirement is not fatal to a claim. There is no time bar as the complaint was filed within the ten-year statute of repose. On the second and third causes of action, the Association is granted leave to allege facts showing damage to the commercial units and that it has the right to maintain claims on behalf of the entities occupying the commercial units. Defendants correctly cite McMillin Albany, LLC v. Super. Crt. (2018) 4 Cal.5th 241 for the proposition that Civil Code 896 and 897 preempt residential common law claims. Fairly read, the complaint is asserting residential claims. In addition, the CC&Rs indicate that those are the only claims that the Association can bring. (CC&Rs 1.3, 1.7, 1.10, 1.11, 1.12, 1.22, 1.26, 1.34, 2.2.) The implied warranty of merchantability claim fails because a condominium or commercial unit is not a "consumer good" that is within the scope of the Song-Beverly Act per Atkinson v Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 757. The unlawful distributions claim fails because the demurring defendants are Delaware entities. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the demurring defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
